info.title: Microsoft identity platform app registration tool
info.description:
This package is a dotnet global tool which registers new Azure AD or Azure AD B2C applications, and updates your code accordingly, or modifies/edits existing
      Azure AD or Azure AD B2C app registrations and updates the project. This tool automates the steps of going to the portal UI for app registration and also updates
      the corresponding code for that application. With this tool, you can develop and register an ASP.NET Core web app, web API, gRPC service or Azure Function 
      protected with the Microsoft identity platform, which can call Microsoft Graph or downstream web APIs.
      For details see https://aka.ms/dotnet-msidentity. Works with ASP.NET Core 3.1 and .NET 5.0, 6.0 projects.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ModelBindingCommandHandler.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Creates or updates an Azure AD / Azure AD B2C application, and updates the project, using your developer credentials (from Visual Studio, Azure CLI, Azure RM PowerShell, VS Code).

options.name: version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: --list-aad-apps
commands.description: Lists AAD Applications for a given tenant/username.

options.name: tenant-id
options.description:
Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant in which to create or update the app registration.
- If specified, the app registration will be created in given tenant.
- If not provided, the app registration will be created in yuor home tenant.

options.name: username
options.description:
Username to use to connect to the Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant.
- It's only needed if you are signed-in to Visual Studio, or Azure CLI with several identities.
- In that case, the username parameter is used to determine which identity to use to create the app registration in the tenant.

options.name: instance
options.description:
Instance where the Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant is located.
If not specified, will default to https://login.microsoftonline.com/

options.name: json
options.description: Format output in JSON instead of text.

options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: --register-app
commands.description:
Register an Azure AD or Azure AD B2C app registration in Azure and update the project.
	- Updates the appsettings.json file.

options.name: tenant-id
options.description:
Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant in which to create or update the app registration.
- If specified, the app registration will be created in given tenant.
- If not provided, the app registration will be created in yuor home tenant.

options.name: username
options.description:
Username to use to connect to the Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant.
- It's only needed if you are signed-in to Visual Studio, or Azure CLI with several identities.
- In that case, the username parameter is used to determine which identity to use to create the app registration in the tenant.

options.name: instance
options.description:
Instance where the Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant is located.
If not specified, will default to https://login.microsoftonline.com/

options.name: json
options.description: Format output in JSON instead of text.

options.name: client-id
options.description:
Client ID of an existing app registration to use.
Configure project to use an existing Azure app registration instead of creating a new one. The app registration will be updated if needed.
- When using this option, you may also need to pass in the client secret with --client-secret.

options.name: client-secret
options.description: Value for the client secret, which also can be referred to as application password.

options.name: hosted-app-id-uri
options.description: The App ID Uri for the Blazor WebAssembly hosted API. This parameter will only be used for Blazor WebAssembly hosted applications.

options.name: api-client-id
options.description:
Client ID of the Blazor WebAssembly hosted web API.
This parameter is only used for Blazor WebAssembly hosted applications, where you only want to configure the project.

options.name: susi-policy-id
options.description: Sign-up/Sign-in policy required for configurating a B2C application from code that was created for Azure AD.

options.name: project-file-path
options.description: Path to the project file (.csproj file) to be used. If not provided, the project file in the current working directory will be used.

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

Unregister an Azure AD or Azure AD B2C app registration in Azure.
	- Updates the appsettings.json file.

options.name: redirect-uris
options.description:
Add redirect URIs (web) for the app.
- You can pass in multiple values for this parameter separated by a space.

options.name: enable-id-token
options.description: Enable id token.

options.name: enable-access-token
options.description: Enable access token.

options.name: client-project
options.description: Path to the project file (.csproj file) for a hosted Blazor WASM client. If provided, implies that project is Blazor WASM Hosted

options.name: api-scopes
options.description: Scopes for the called downstream API, especially useful for B2C scenarios where permissions must be granted manually

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: --update-project
commands.description:
Update an Azure AD/AD B2C app registration in Azure and the project.
	- Updates the appsettings.json file.
	- Updates the Startup.cs file.
	- Updates the user secrets.

options.name: tenant-id
options.description:
Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant in which to create or update the app registration.
- If specified, the app registration will be created in given tenant.
- If not provided, the app registration will be created in yuor home tenant.

options.name: username
options.description:
Username to use to connect to the Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant.
- It's only needed if you are signed-in to Visual Studio, or Azure CLI with several identities.
- In that case, the username parameter is used to determine which identity to use to create the app registration in the tenant.

options.name: instance
options.description:
Instance where the Azure AD or Azure AD B2C tenant is located.
If not specified, will default to https://login.microsoftonline.com/

options.name: client-id
options.description:
Client ID of an existing app registration to use.
Configure project to use an existing Azure app registration instead of creating a new one. The app registration will be updated if needed.
- When using this option, you may also need to pass in the client secret with --client-secret.

options.name: json
options.description: Format output in JSON instead of text.

options.name: project-file-path
options.description: Path to the project file (.csproj file) to be used. If not provided, the project file in the current working directory will be used.

options.name: config-update
options.description: Allow config changes for dotnet app to work with Azure AD/AD B2C app.

options.name: code-update
options.description: Allow Startup.cs and other code changes for dotnet app to work with Azure AD/AD B2C app (setup authentication).

options.name: packages-update
options.description: Allow package updates for dotnet app to work with Azure AD/AD B2C app (setup authentication).

options.name: calls-graph
options.description: App registration calls microsoft graph.

options.name: calls-downstream-api
options.description: App registration calls downstream api.

options.name: update-user-secrets
options.description:
Add secrets to user secrets.json file.
- Using dotnet-user-secrets to init and set user secrets.

options.name: redirect-uris
options.description:
Add redirect URIs (web) for the app.
- You can pass in multiple values for this parameter separated by a space.

options.name: susi-policy-id
options.description: Sign-up/Sign-in policy required for configurating a B2C application from code that was created for Azure AD.

options.name: called-api-url
options.description: URL of the called downstream API

options.name: api-scopes
options.description: Scopes for the called downstream API, especially useful for B2C scenarios where permissions must be granted manually

options.name: help
options.description: Show help and usage information
